programme.desc: John Oliver sheds light on recent Supreme Court cases and discusses gay rights in Uganda with activist Pepe Julian Onziema.

programme.desc: It's New Year's Eve 2005 and Ted spends a large portion of his Christmas bonus for a limo for him and the gang. In addition, he has outlined a plan for how they will ring in the New Year. Unfortunately, things don't always go as planned.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Robin accepts Ted's invite to a friend's wedding, but thanks to Ted, the couple may never make it down the aisle.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: New Girl

programme.desc: Sheldon finds himself booted from the gang's Physics Bowl team and replaced with Leslie, so he forms his own team, hoping to prove he's superior.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy's ongoing bet about who can make more arrests comes to a close. Charles receives the Medal of Valor for getting shot in the line of duty, but the pain medication he is taking causes him to reveal his true opinions.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The show returns with another group of hapless comedians given some fiendishly tricky tasks. In this opening episode these include vegetable propulsion, Scoopy the out-of-season snowman and some hot swede on Swede action.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John talks about Gaza (but mostly Anderson Cooper) and about Malaysia Airlines flight 17, then discusses prison incarcerations in America and sings with Muppet-like puppets, similar to those from Sesame Street.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: At a Chili's dinner with a potential client, Michael's antics, Jan's professionalism, and an impromptu "Baby Back Ribs" duet unexpectedly close the deal, while Dwight and Kevin read Jim's top-secret document.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael expects praise but receives month-old evaluation notes; his whirlwind performance review includes Dwight's compliments, Jan's disapproval, and a parking-lot rant that leaves Pam and Jim hopelessly amused.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie enlists the help of Dave and Andy in order to catch a local teen vandal. Meanwhile, Ann struggles to make her Halloween party fun, and gets help from an unlikely source.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie is upset when she learns the library department wants to take over her lot. Making matters worse, the library is run by Ron's ex-wife Tammy, who still has a powerful emotional hold on him. Meanwhile, Andy gets a job in hopes of winning Ann back.

programme.title: Utopia (AU)
programme.desc: After months of working on a major new project Jim asks Tony for one small change. He wants the project killed. Rhonda nominates Nat to be keynote speaker at a conference.

programme.title: Utopia (AU)
programme.desc: Tony refuses to be drawn into a planning dispute. Meanwhile, Rhonda is keen to raise the NBA's level of online engagement, leading to social media mayhem.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.sub-title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner, Honey
programme.desc: After learning Benny had a one-night fling with a co-worker 35 years ago, George believes he has finally found his father. He meets Lalo - whose picture bears a striking resemblance to George -- and invites him over for Thanksgiving dinner. But George's bond is tested when he learns the truth.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.desc: When Angie finds she has more free time around the house, she volunteers with a variety of charities. Feeling invincible, she soon finds herself joining more organizations than she can handle, and it's up to George and the family to help her get back on track.

programme.title: Everybody Hates Chris
programme.desc: Chris tries to put together the right outfit for the school pictures in order to convince the other kids that he's cool. Rochelle tries to earn extra money selling cosmetics from home.

programme.title: Everybody Hates Chris
programme.desc: Chris gets a bunch of "I hate you" cards from Caruso and also develops a crush on a girl named Tangee on his bus ride. Tonya reveals that she has a boyfriend which surprises Julius, and Drew gets a bunch of Valentines' cards from a lot of girls.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny finds a potential buyer for the town, while David and Alexis discover they may have become more attached to Schitt’s Creek than they ever thought possible.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The Roses search for their missing son, David, while Alexis takes her relationship with Mutt to the next level despite technically being engaged to Ted.

programme.title: The Naked Gun
programme.desc: Only one man has the particular set of skills... to lead Police Squad and save the world: Lt. Frank Drebin Jr. Following in his father's footsteps, he must solve a murder case to prevent Police Squad from closure.

programme.title: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die
programme.desc: A "Man From the Future" arrives at a diner in Los Angeles where he must recruit the precise combination of disgruntled patrons to join him on a one-night quest to save the world from the terminal threat of a rogue artificial intelligence.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Both Autobots and Decepticons are facing tough times as they are in need of the element called Cybertonium. The Decepticons quickly get as much as they need, but the only way for the Autobots to get it is to rely on The Dinobots, who have been unaffected by all this. However, the Dinobots have been getting annoyed with The Autobots lately and leave them.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Both Autobots and Decepticons are facing tough times as they are in need of the element called Cybertonium. The Decepticons quickly get as much as they need, but the only way for the Autobots to get it is to rely on the Dinobots, who have been unaffected by all this. However, the Dinobots have been getting annoyed with the Autobots lately and leave them.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Archer has been defeated by the unbeatable Beserker and now Shirou, Saber and Rin are trying their best to escape the same fate. They are able to find shelter to bide some time, because Shirou has to find a way to replenish Saber's mana while Ilya looks for them. With Archer's sacrifice, Rin is that much more determined to get out of this alive and comes up with an elaborate plan. The beginning of a fight between Saber and Berserker commences.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: JoJo's Bizarre Adventure
programme.desc: Hostility grows between Jonathan and Dio over the years, and forgotten secrets emerge. Jonathan is suspicious about the secret to the mask, but Dio has already prepared to take him out of the picture with the help of a London street thug.

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The world's most boring priest comes to stay, and he just won't go away. After his worst ever birthday party, Father Ted prays for deliverance from Father Stone, but a lightning strike wasn't what he expected.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: The four bunk off School and get drunk. Simon tells Carli D'amato he loves her by writing it on her drive way in big letters. It ends badly when Simon and Will's parents find out what they've been doing all day.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Capt. Mainwaring, worried about the lack of weapons, gets a visit from Col. Square, an old soldier who offers to donate some guns from his collection, providing he can take command of the platoon. Mainwaring agrees until he sees the state of the guns.

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Sessions by Fanny and Elton John.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross will fascinate you with this crimson winter scene, where cabin and snow are virtually undisturbed and the evergreens are full.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Rarity goes to Manehattan for Fashion Week and loses her sense of generosity after another designer copies her entire fashion line.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: The students of Twilight's School of Friendship are cramming for "History of Magic in Equestria" exam in the school library when they discover a part of the school that no pony else knows about. When they decide to do some exploring they end up learning much more than they bargained for.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: A battle with the Decepticons leaves Optimus Prime damaged. Several Autobots journey to Cybertron to retrieve a vital part that will help repair him, but the Decepticons take advantage of the situation and launch an attack.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: New allies for the Autobots are built, bringing about Grimlock, Slag and Sludge. However, the Dinobots prove hazardous to the Autobots and must be locked away. But they may be the only hope for the Autobots after a battle with the Decepticons.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Will persuades Ashley to pawn her violin for a drum set.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Murr and Joe use a two-way mirror to get each other to crack, and then hit a grocery to see who can sell the most samples. Plus, tonight's loser gets taken for a ride that ends with a visit to the Statue of Liberty.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Capt. Mainwaring is giving a lecture on recognising enemy agents when he learns that for every Nazi captured there is a reward. Later on Jones and Walker capture two Luftwaffe airmen, although a Polish officer intends to claim the reward. Back at HQ the airmen escape on Godfrey's watch.

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: With his father away at the Crusades, Edmund comes up with a plan to prove his brother is illegitimate, thus making him Prince Regent.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are wary of Anais' new friend, Josh, making her question his motives as well.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a music composer realise her dream of escaping suburbia for the Shropshire countryside, on a budget of £450,000. Away from the house hunt, Nicki visits a stately home which is raising the roof.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy decides to get a flock of sheep, not realising the added complications these high-maintenance animals will bring.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: Britain's famous Lake District is where tourists are plentiful and money is flowing. However, the Glass House Restaurant in Ambleside seems to have missed both points.

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc: Throughout Golden Week, Tsukasa and Kagami have been playing together. However, Tsukasa does not know that her sister has actually been waking up earlier and going to bed later than her in order to study and finish homework. After the break, Konata does an all-nighter and manages to obtain an above-average score despite doing no studying during the short vacation. Later, Konata decides to get a job at a cosplay café in order to fund her otaku desires.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry and Alan Davies are joined by Clive Anderson, Bill Bailey and Meera Syal to discuss some Quite Interesting topics beginning with the letter A including the Longest Animal, Blue Whales, Octupus, Antartica, AmlAnnoying.com, John Henry Anderson, Hans Christian Anderson, Atlas and Oxygen.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the almost impossible quiz show with guests Alan Davies, Jo Brand, Howard Goodall and Jeremy Hardy. The theme of this edition of the show is atoms and questions range from Argentina to the composition of air.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A girl's unimaginable bravery toward her terminal illness makes House suspicious as he puts a life-or-death decision about her case in her young hands.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin search for adventure during a walk around Elmore.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard drives the Ford Focus. Nick Mason, Pink Floyd's drummer, lends his Ferrari F60 Enzo to Jeremy. A teenager on a skateboard races the Bowler Wildcat down a grassy track. Geri Halliwell is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: Karl travels to India and gives own unique verdict on its ancient customs and vibrant culture.

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After a huge misunderstanding on the phone, Stacey thinks Gavin has finished with her. He drives to Wales to explain and ends up taking her home with him. Having never been further than Bridgend before, her trip to Essex is an eye-opening experience that ends with the biggest surprise of all.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: Martha takes her first trip in the Tardis, all the way back to Elizabethan England. She and the Doctor hear of a series of bizarre deaths and discover that playwright William Shakespeare is being controlled by dangerous witch-like creatures. It seems the time-travelling duo must defeat ancient forces to prevent history from being rewritten.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Gerald owns a beautiful house with lots of beautiful things in it. Beautiful things that are also extremely valuable. The kind of things that might just become the target of an audacious criminal enterprise.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: W.P.C. Ackland and P.C. Smith attend a local gypsy campsite where residents are complaining. A social worker has a Place Of Safety Order on one of the gypsy children, Theresa Beecher, who was reportedly beaten by her parents. When the girl and her parents lock themselves in the caravan, Smith has the idea of towing the caravan to the station. A youth is found in possession of stolen credit cards, and the owner is located and confirms he was attacked and robbed the night before. He is about to do an identity parade, but his wife admits she was the one who hit him. The gypsies make a run for it, but when the little girl is taken into care, it is found the Beechers have swapped their daughter with another little girl.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Ch. Insp. Conway is late for a meeting with a Mr. Trevelyan from the Department of Trade and Industry, who wants police help in shutting down Radio Sun Hill, a pirate radio station. Conway orders the winos who blocked his car taken off the streets, but when one of them tries to escape from his cell, Sgt. Cryer recognises him as an undercover cop. P.C. Smith asks W.P.C. Martella to talk to a lady who has been beaten up by her boyfriend. She changes her mind about pressing charges as she doesn't want her husband to know. She then explains the injuries by saying the police beat her up, and her husband turns up and attacks Yorkie. Despite D.S. Roach's objections that it's a waste of police time, Carver and Dashwood raid Radio Sun Hill, but they escape.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: P.C. Stamp and W.P.C. Brind arrest a man who is so drunk he can barely stand. Back at the station, the man collapses after a drug overdose, but not before implying that he's killed a woman. D.C. Lines and D.C. Carver race to find the man's identity through his dental records and the local jazz clubs, but the man's wife (and his mistress) are found safe and well. P.C. Melvin is sent to inform the wife of a man killed in a factory accident, but he radios in when he suspects that he has told the wrong person. D.C. Dashwood checks at the factory, and finds it was a similarly-named man who died, and the very-much-alive husband arrives at the station to make a formal complaint.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: From the moon, Megatron manages to interfere with the radio transmissions on Earth. Blaster and Cosmos go to put an end to his plot as the other Autobots work to help those affected by the loss of radio transmissions.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.sub-title: A Decepticon Raider in King Arthur's Court
programme.desc: Spike, Hoist and Warpath find their battle with a group of Decepticons interrupted when they all get sent back in time to the middle ages.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Archer has been defeated by the unbeatable Beserker and now Shirou, Saber and Rin are trying their best to escape the same fate. They are able to find shelter to bide some time, because Shirou has to find a way to replenish Saber's mana while Ilya looks for them. With Archer's sacrifice, Rin is that much more determined to get out of this alive and comes up with an elaborate plan. The beginning of a fight between Saber and Berserker commences.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: JoJo's Bizarre Adventure
programme.desc: JoJo prepares to turn Dio in to the police.

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The local cinema is showing a film condemned by the Church for its X-rated and blasphemous depiction of Saint Tibulus. Bishop Brennan is dispatched to the island to ensure that the priests take to the picket line in protest. But their defiant stand only gives more publicity to the film, which becomes an overnight success. It is left to Father Jack to stop the Bishop from sending them packing.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon passes his driving test at the first attempt, less on account of his skills than the fact that Tracey, the saucy examiner, finds him cute. As promised, he agrees to take his three friends to Thorpe Park, where he can get in free as he knows the man who clears up the sick. Once on the road, Will, Jay and Neil realize that Simon is a rubbish driver. (Until The Happy Foundation Shows Up)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: The Showing Up of Corporal Jones
programme.desc: Just when the platoon finally gets its uniforms, LCpl. Jones is told that unless he can complete the assault course in 15 minutes he will have to leave, so Mainwaring and the rest of the platoon think of a plan to help him.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Feel the cool breeze and admire the beauty of turbulent waves, jagged cliffs and dark night skies -- Bob Ross paints a west coast seascape.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: When Fluttershy and Angel Bunny's relationship hits the skids, Zecora gives them a potion to help them understand each other.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Fluttershy is determined to make her career dreams come true with the help of some very special ponies. But when Fluttershy's vision is not being executed properly, she must find a way to stand up and fight for her ideas.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: The Decepticons raid the Ark after tricking the Autobots with a distraction and Sparkplug gets captured. They use a device called a hypno-chip, created by a human named Dr. Arkeville, on Sparkplug to gain control over him. It is all apart of an evil plan to get a work force to create a new space bridge and bring Cybertron to Earth.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: With Cybertron inside Earth's orbit, the Earth starts to erupt into chaos. The Decepticons gather more human slaves to prepare to send their Energon cubes to Cybertron. While the Dinobots protect Earth, the Autobots head for Cybertron.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Will wants to go out with a beautiful girl he meets at the country club, but first he must figure out how to impress her extremely snobbish and very protective father so he seeks tips from cousin Carlton on becoming preppie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Sal, Joe, Murr and Q get stuck in a grocery showdown, beg strangers for questionable quotes, and spew financial tips that are anything but sound. Plus, tonight's loser earns a time-out for his outrageous antics at a kid's play space.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: When news that the Prime Minister is to pay a visit to the area a shooting contest is held to see which of the local platoons will form the Guard of Honour. Walker gets a crack shot from the theatre touring show (Barbara Windsor) to take part as a member of the platoon but a surprise is in store for everybody.

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed to the post of Archbishop of Canterbury after the king has the last one killed.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After finding a secret manifesto on Alan's memory stick, Gumball and Darwin decide to foil his plan for world domination.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc:
Nicki Chapman is helping a couple find their first rural property together in Devon. The buyers have a budget of £400,000 to spend on their long-dreamed-of country home.
Away from the house hunt, Nicki encounters the prehistoric fish behind the world's most luxurious food.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson decides to open a farm shop to sell his own produce. This sounds like it should be easy. It isn't.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: In a previous life, The Walnut Tree Inn in South Wales was a Michelin-starred restaurant known throughout Britain. Under its new owners, Italian Francesco Mattioli, Gordon arrives to find the kitchen in chaos with no Head Chef and no customers.

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc: It is summertime for the girls; that means one thing: comfortable summer uniforms! Tsukasa and Kagami are fraternal twins, but Konata shows them the similar and different characteristics they share. Konata, Kagami, and Tsukasa each try to put their hair into a ponytail, just to see who looks best. Unfortunately, Kagami is the only one who cannot pull it off.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry and Alan Davies are joined by Gyles Brandreth, Rob Brydon and Rich Hall to discuss some Quite Interesting topics beginning with the letter A.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry and Alan Davies are joined by Danny Baker, Jo Brand and Howard Goodall to discuss some Quite Interesting topics beginning with the letter A.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Cuddy pushes her asthmatic handyman Alfredo to continue working. He falls and badly wounds his hand. Alas the infection spreads and causes necrosis while House and she bicker over the possible causes and risky treatments, which keep making it worse. Even two house searches don't clarify matters. Alfredo must keep several poor jobs to put his kid brother, Manny, through school, which has a surprising relevance.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole reminisces about how she met Richard during a disastrous family dinner, and how things could have turned out for her if she had not.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy goes to Scotland to road test the new Land Rover Discovery by attempting to drive the Discovery to the top of the mountain called Mount Cnoc an Fhreiceadain. Richard travels to Switzerland to find the maddest car ever made. Jeremy test the new Dodge Viper SRT-10

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: This week, Karl Pilkington's voyage takes him to Petra in Jordan, by way of Israel. What will he make of the 'rose-red city half as old as time'?

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After his initial angry reaction to Stacey's news, Uncle Bryn agrees to drive everyone to Essex to meet the Shipmans. Pam has laid on a cracking spread and the party goes with a bang, but then Gavin and Stacey make an announcement that throws all the bonhomie out the window..

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: The TARDIS takes the Doctor and Martha to New Earth in the year Five Billion and Forty-Three, where the Face of Boe finally reveals his message to the Time Lord; meanwhile, terrifying beasts are lurking beneath the city of New New York - creatures that the Doctor believed he defeated many, many years ago.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Tom is a primary school teacher with ambitious ideas of becoming an author. When he tries to repay a good turn from a late-night caller, he gradually becomes the victim of his own generosity.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: W.P.C. Brind and P.C. Melvin are on foot patrol, but are seperated. Brind ends up dealing with a workplace dispute by herself, but the ringleader, Nobby Briggs, smashes her radio and locks her in a storeroom. When Melvin eventually turns up, he calls for assistance and Briggs is arrested. Sgt. Cryer is in a bad mood as he is suffering from a painful toothache, and he yells at a prisoner and pushes him. Lay visitors from the council arrive to inspect the station, and the prisoner accuses Cryer of assaulting him. His cellmate eventually clears Cryer, but he has to cancel his dentist appointment when he is called up to Ch. Supt Brownlow's office over the incident.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: D.S. Roach goes to the hospital to talk to a young man who was slashed in the face with a knife - the fifth is a series of ""steamer"" attacks, where knife-wielding youths have held up restaurant patrons. D.C. Dashwood receives a tip-off about the steamers' next target, and the restuarant is filled with undercover police officers. P.C. Edwards is jealous that he and Carver didn't get a free meal paid for by the Met, and the steamers are caught when they raid the restuarant. P.C. Melvin investigates a strange smell from a flat used by junkies, and finds the body of an elderly man who suffered a heart attack. Melvin can't believe the old man was a junkie.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: P.C. Haynes volunteers for ""duty elsewhere"", undercover as a driver for Neville Tubbs, leader of a Yardie gang in South London. A man has just been murdered and mutilated, and Rita, a girl from Tubbs' club tells Haynes she witnessed the killing. Gaining Tubbs' trust, Haynes is asked to transport a package to a meeting across town. He is stopped by two police officers, but manages to get away after telling them he's in the Job. Haynes delivers the package, but is recognised as a cop by a Brixton Yardie. The police raid the place before Haynes is hurt or killed, but he is horrified to discover the package he carried contains the murder victim's hands.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Astrotrain tricks a race living on one of Saturn's moons, Titan, into thinking he is a god and gets them to do his bidding, which includes getting a hold of a newly discovered power.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Megatron plans to drill his way to Earth's core in hopes of getting large amounts of energy. The Autobots try to stop him and initiate their new plan to take control over Devastator. When both sides battle for controlling Devastator, though, it causes disaster.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Archer has been defeated by the unbeatable Beserker and now Shirou, Saber and Rin are trying their best to escape the same fate. programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: JoJo's Bizarre Adventure
programme.desc: Jonathan wakes up days later heavily bandaged and learns Dio is not dead, but a mysterious man appears and offers to teach Jonathan the magic technique of Hamon that will help him defeat Dio for good.

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: It's the 'All Priests Stars In Their Eyes Lookalike Competition', and showbusiness personality Henry Sellers, a former alcoholic, has landed on the island to host the festivities. Father Ted is determined not to go as Mother Teresa again, but he isn't the only one to think of Elvis. It takes some quick thinking to beat Father Byrne's team from nearby Rugged Island.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: To the amazement - and annoyance - of the other three boys, Will wins a Blind Date competition which pairs him off with Charlotte Hinchcliffe, the most desired girl in the school, and he even ends up in bed with her though he keeps his socks on.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: When six members of the Highland Regiment attack the Warmington-on-Sea's HQ, the platoon has to come up with a scheme to capture the attackers.
